title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After being offered a cocktail, Countess Razkai recounts her experience to Detective Bartlett, Detective Bündel, Bill Everly, and others. She explains that unable to sleep due to the oppressive atmosphere of the house, she went downstairs with a small flashlight to find a book. While searching the bookshelf, she heard stealthy footsteps and hid behind a Spanish leather screen. From her hiding place, she witnessed a struggle between a man with a rough voice, who repeatedly pleaded "Let me go," and a gentleman with a distinct British accent. During the fight, a gunshot was fired, after which the Countess believes she fainted.

Inspector Bartlett investigates the scene and discovers a .45 caliber shell casing near the bookshelf. He determines that the shell hit the bookshelf while the bullet ricocheted off a window frame and flew outside. Jimmy Sycamore, who has a bandaged arm, admits to being the gentleman with the British accent. Inspector Bartlett explains that the incident was an attempted theft of political documents belonging to Sir Stanley Digby, which was thwarted by the presence of a young girl named Loran.

As the Countess leaves the room leaning on Bill's arm, Bündel observes a small black mole on the Countess's right shoulder blade through her thin nightgown. Meanwhile, Inspector Bartlett prepares to secure the room. George Lomax and Dr. Carter arrive, with the doctor noting that Mr. Orock has been given an injection and will wake in the morning. Jimmy asks Loran to assist him as he prepares to have his gunshot wound examined.
